paper_title: Experimental Procedures for Maize and Arabidopsis thaliana Protein Studies

methods.method_name: Expression and Purification of Recombinant Arabidopsis thaliana CaM Isoforms
methods.description: This method describes the expression and purification of recombinant CaM isoforms from Arabidopsis thaliana using E. coli expression systems.

steps.description: E. coli BL21(DE3) cells containing the recombinant pET CaM expression clone were grown to A 600 of 0.6 and induced by 1 mM IPTG for 3 h at 37°C in 1 liter of NZY medium containing 50 g/ml ampicillin.
steps.searchable_summary: Grow E. coli cells and induce protein expression with IPTG

steps.description: The cells were harvested, washed in buffer A (50 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.5), and resuspended in extraction buffer (Buffer A with 2 mM EDTA, 1 mM DTT, 200 g/ml lysozyme, and complete protease inhibitor mixture).
steps.searchable_summary: Harvest and wash cells, resuspend in extraction buffer

steps.description: After treatment with DNase to remove DNA, the cell extract was clarified by centrifugation and the supernatant fraction was precipitated with 55% ammonium sulfate.
steps.searchable_summary: Treat with DNase, centrifuge, and precipitate proteins with ammonium sulfate

steps.description: The proteins in the supernatant were precipitated with 50% H2SO4 (pH 4) for 30 min with stirring.
steps.searchable_summary: Precipitate proteins with sulfuric acid

steps.description: After centrifugation, the pellet was resuspended in buffer A containing 1 mM DTT, dialyzed first in distilled water, and then in buffer A containing 100 mM NaCl, 0.5 mM EGTA, and 1 mM DTT.
steps.searchable_summary: Resuspend pellet, dialyze in water and buffer

steps.description: After adjusting CaCl2 concentration to 5 mM, the protein was loaded onto a phenyl-Sepharose column CL-4B (10 ml bed volume) pre-equilibrated with buffer B (buffer A containing 0.1 mM CaCl2 and 0.5 mM DTT).
steps.searchable_summary: Load protein onto phenyl-Sepharose column

steps.description: The column was washed with buffer B containing 5 mM NaCl and the AtCaM protein was eluted with elution buffer containing 50 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.5, 1 mM EGTA, and 0.5 mM DTT.
steps.searchable_summary: Wash and elute protein from column

steps.description: The eluates containing the proteins were dialyzed in water.
steps.searchable_summary: Dialyze eluted proteins in water

methods.method_name: Production and Purification of 35 S-labeled AtCaM Isoforms
methods.description: This method outlines the production and purification of 35 S-labeled AtCaM isoforms for use in binding studies.

steps.description: Cells were grown in M9 medium with 10 g/liter tryptone and 50 g/ml ampicillin overnight and then concentrated in 10 ml of M9 medium with ampicillin.
steps.searchable_summary: Grow and concentrate cells in M9 medium

steps.description: One mM IPTG was added to the cells (0.6 A 600), 2 mCi of Easy tag 35 S-labeling mixture was added after 15 min, and the cultures were grown for 3 h at 37°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Induce protein expression and label with 35 S

steps.description: The cells were pelleted, resuspended in 1.5 ml of buffer A, lysed with lysozyme (0.2 mg/ml), and DNase-treated (50 units) in the presence of 3 mM MgCl2.
steps.searchable_summary: Pellet, resuspend, lyse, and DNase-treat cells

steps.description: After centrifuging the lysate at 30,000 rpm for 30 min, the supernatant was heated for 3 min at 90°C, centrifuged, and the resulting supernatant was used to purify the radiolabeled CaM on a 1-ml phenyl-Sepharose CL-4B column.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge, heat, and purify radiolabeled CaM

methods.method_name: Screening of Maize Pollen Expression Library with 35 S-CaM
methods.description: This method involves screening a maize pollen cDNA library using 35 S-labeled AtCaM isoforms to identify potential binding partners.

steps.description: About 120,000 recombinants were screened with a mixture of 35 S-labeled AtCaM isoforms 4 and 6.
steps.searchable_summary: Screen recombinants with 35 S-labeled CaM

steps.description: Approximately 9000 pfu per 15-cm plate were plated on NZCYM plates using E. coli XL1-blue MRA as the host strain.
steps.searchable_summary: Plate recombinants on NZCYM plates

steps.description: The plates were incubated at 42°C until the plaques appeared, at which time the plates were overlaid with nitrocellulose filters soaked in 10 mM IPTG.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate plates and overlay with nitrocellulose filters

steps.description: Plaques were allowed to resume growth overnight at 37°C. The plates were then placed at 4°C for 1 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Allow plaques to grow and cool plates

steps.description: The nitrocellulose filters were removed and washed briefly in TBS.
steps.searchable_summary: Wash nitrocellulose filters in TBS

steps.description: The filters were blocked in TBS containing 1% nonfat milk for 15 min with gentle shaking at room temperature and then incubated in a mixture of 35 S-labeled At-CaM isoforms at 5 g/ml for 12 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Block filters and incubate with 35 S-labeled CaM

steps.description: The membranes were rinsed in TBS/Ca 2+ three times for 5 min each and dried between 2 sheets of 3MM paper for 24 h before exposing to a x-ray film.
steps.searchable_summary: Rinse membranes and prepare for x-ray exposure

steps.description: The putative positive plaques were plaque-purified by two additional rounds of screening.
steps.searchable_summary: Plaque-purify positive plaques

steps.description: The cDNA inserts from Zap II were excised in vivo in a plasmid form using the Exassist helper phage and by infecting E. coli SOLR cells with phage recombinant.
steps.searchable_summary: Excise cDNA inserts using Exassist phage

steps.description: The insert was excised by digesting the plasmid DNA with EcoRI.
steps.searchable_summary: Digest plasmid DNA with EcoRI

steps.description: The positive clones were confirmed for Ca 2+ dependence in binding to CaM by expressing the purified clones as above, incubating the membrane in 35 S-CaM probe, and washing the membrane in TBS buffer containing either 2 mM Ca 2+ or 5 mM EGTA.
steps.searchable_summary: Confirm Ca 2+ dependence of clones

methods.description: This method describes the isolation of a genomic clone from a maize genomic library using a 32 P-labeled cDNA probe.

steps.description: Maize genomic library in EMBL3 was screened with a 32 P-labeled cDNA (1.2 kb) according to the standard.
steps.searchable_summary: Screen genomic library with 32 P-labeled cDNA

steps.description: About 650,000 recombinants were screened and about 20 positives were isolated after two rounds of screening.
steps.searchable_summary: Isolate positive recombinants

steps.description: Restriction mapping and Southern analysis of the positive clones indicated that all of the isolated clones were derived from the same gene.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform restriction mapping and Southern analysis

steps.description: The clone with the largest hybridizing fragment was sequenced by primer walking.
steps.searchable_summary: Sequence clone by primer walking

methods.description: This method involves sequencing and analyzing cDNA using dideoxynucleotide chain termination and bioinformatics tools.

steps.description: Both strands of cDNAs were sequenced by dideoxynucleotide chain termination using double stranded DNA.
steps.searchable_summary: Sequence cDNA using dideoxynucleotide chain termination

steps.description: Sequence analysis was performed using MacVector and Sequencher programs.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze sequence data with MacVector and Sequencher

steps.description: BLAST searches were performed at the National Center for Biotechnology Information web site.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform BLAST searches for sequence comparison

methods.method_name: Construction and Expression of Truncated cDNAs in E. coli
methods.description: This method describes the construction and expression of truncated cDNA constructs in E. coli to map the CaM-binding domain.

steps.description: The 1.2-kb cDNA insert (named P3) containing the coding region for 242 amino acids in the carboxyl-terminal region was cloned in-frame into pET 28b expression vector.
steps.searchable_summary: Clone cDNA insert into pET 28b vector

steps.description: For mapping the CaM-binding domain, several truncated constructs were prepared in pET vector.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare truncated constructs in pET vector

steps.description: The P3 cDNA in pET 28 vector was digested with either NcoI or BamHI to release the 168-or 717-bp fragment corresponding to the 5Ј end of the cDNA.
steps.searchable_summary: Digest cDNA with NcoI or BamHI

steps.description: The NcoI and BamHI fragments were cloned into pET 28b vector digested with respective enzymes to generate P3/NcoI and P3/BamHI clones.
steps.searchable_summary: Clone digested fragments into pET 28b vector

steps.description: The P3 clones lacking either NcoI or BamHI fragments were religated to generate P⌬NcoI and P⌬BamHI expression clones.
steps.searchable_summary: Religate clones to generate expression constructs

steps.description: A 531-bp fragment representing the BamHI fragment without the NcoI fragment was also cloned in pET 28b vector.
steps.searchable_summary: Clone BamHI fragment into pET 28b vector

steps.description: All the clones were introduced into E. coli BL21(DE3) and expressed.
steps.searchable_summary: Introduce and express clones in E. coli

steps.description: Expression of the protein was achieved by growing the bacterial cells containing the subclones at 37°C to an A 600 of 0.6, IPTG was then added to the cultures to a final concentration of 1 mM and the cells were allowed to grow for 3 more hours at 30°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Induce protein expression with IPTG

steps.description: The cells from induced and uninduced cultures were collected, resuspended in lysis buffer, vortexed, and incubated on ice for 1 h.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect and lyse cells

steps.description: The mixture was then sonicated 3 times, 10 s each, using a Virsonic digital 475 ultrasonic cell disruptor.
steps.searchable_summary: Sonicate cell mixture

steps.description: The lysate was then centrifuged at 12,000 ϫ g for 20 min to separate the soluble (supernatant) from the insoluble (pellet) fractions.
steps.searchable_summary: Centrifuge lysate to separate fractions

steps.description: The pellet was dissolved in either 6 M urea containing buffer or sample buffer, and the supernatant and the pellet were electrophoresed on 12% SDS-polyacrylamide gels.
steps.searchable_summary: Dissolve pellet and perform SDS-PAGE

steps.description: The gels were blotted onto a nitrocellulose membrane using a Bio-Rad transfer cell.
steps.searchable_summary: Blot gels onto nitrocellulose membrane

steps.description: Expressed proteins were detected by T7 tag monoclonal antibody conjugated to alkaline phosphatase.
steps.searchable_summary: Detect proteins with T7 tag antibody

methods.description: This method describes the detection of calmodulin binding to fusion proteins using radiolabeled and biotinylated calmodulin.

steps.description: Soluble and insoluble proteins from the induced and uninduced cultures were separated on 12% SDS-polyacrylamide gels and blotted as described above.
steps.searchable_summary: Separate and blot proteins on SDS-PAGE

steps.description: To detect the binding of the expressed proteins to 35 S-labeled AtCaM, duplicate blots were blocked for 15 min in TBS containing 5 mM of either CaCl2 or EGTA and 1% nonfat dry milk.
steps.searchable_summary: Block blots and prepare for 35 S-CaM binding

steps.description: The blots were then incubated for 12 h in a mixture of 35 S-labeled AtCaM isoforms at 5 g/ml in the same buffers as above.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate blots with 35 S-labeled CaM

steps.description: The filters were then washed with the corresponding buffer and dried before exposing to x-ray film.
steps.searchable_summary: Wash and dry filters for x-ray exposure

steps.description: Binding to biotinylated CaM was detected by blocking the blots in 3% gelatin in TBS/Ca/Mg or TBS/EGTA/Mg at 30°C.
steps.searchable_summary: Block blots for biotinylated CaM binding

steps.description: The blots were then incubated in 5 g/ml biotinylated CaM for 2 h in TBS/Ca/Mg or TBS/EGTA/Mg containing 0.1% Tween 20 and 1% gelatin followed by three washes of 10 min each in the corresponding buffers above.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate blots with biotinylated CaM

steps.description: The blots were then incubated in Vectastain ABC.HRP in TBS/Ca/Mg or TBS/EGTA/Mg for 30 min, washed 3 times, 10 min each, with the above buffer and the CaM-binding proteins were detected colorimetrically in a substrate solution.
steps.searchable_summary: Detect CaM-binding proteins colorimetrically
